name: main-question
text:
Can semantic remapping hide
from a text-only auditor?
textPreview: Can semantic remapping hide | from a text-only auditor?

name: scope
text: Doublespeak MVP  •  3 OpenAI models  •  10 remapping attacks + 10 matched clean controls per model
textPreview: Doublespeak MVP  •  3 OpenAI models  •  10 remapping attacks + 10 matched clean controls per model

name: definition
text: Blind spot: the target adopts the hidden meaning, while an independent auditor misses it.
textPreview: Blind spot: the target adopts the hidden meaning, while an independent auditor misses it.

title: Each example was tested twice—with no response sharing

name: method-title
text: Each example was tested twice—with no response sharing
textPreview: Each example was tested twice—with no response sharing

name: method-subtitle
text: Same model, temperature 0, fresh API calls
textPreview: Same model, temperature 0, fresh API calls

name: step-1
text:
Target semantic check

Does the model interpret the benign substitute as the intended harmful concept?
textPreview: Target semantic check |  | Does the model interpret the benign substitute as the intended harmful concept?

name: step-2
text:
Independent text auditor

A fresh call sees only the prompt. It detects and attempts to recover the mapping.
textPreview: Independent text auditor |  | A fresh call sees only the prompt. It detects and attempts to recover the mapping.

name: step-3
text:
Blind-spot test

Target adopted mapping
AND
auditor did not detect it
textPreview: Blind-spot test |  | Target adopted mapping | AND | auditor did not detect it

name: call-count
text: Per model: 20 examples × 2 independent calls = 40 primary API calls
textPreview: Per model: 20 examples × 2 independent calls = 40 primary API calls

name: interpretation-copy
text: The remappings succeeded—but the text-only auditor caught every one. Harder attacks are needed before testing whether internal-state monitoring adds signal.
